blocks.heading: The shared block→HTML rendering engine every generator builds on — define a typed `Block[]`, hand it to one `assemble()`, get a self-contained HTML document back.

rows.value: `src` (the engine modules) + `src/renderers/*` (one renderer per block type).
Owns the `Block` union and the single render pipeline. Every product (recap, atlas, spec, doc) is just a different array of these blocks fed to `assemble()`.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "Every product is just a different array of the same Block union, rendered by one assemble()."
bigIdea.sub: There is no per-product render path. A product's whole job is to build a `Block[]` — diagrams, diffs, prose, file trees, annotated code — and `assemble()` walks it, dispatches each block to its renderer by a `switch (b.type)`, inlines the CSS + viewer JS, and returns one portable HTML string. Add a capability once here and all four generators get it.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: A typed block model at the center, one `assemble()` that walks it into an HTML shell, a fan-out of per-type renderers, and three helpers (highlight, lint, version). Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: block-model
cards.purpose: The `Block` discriminated union — the contract between products and the engine. Every renderable thing (diagram, diff, prose, api, file-tree, annotated-code, questions, group, tabs, overview) is one variant, keyed by `type`.

cards.name: assemble + shell
cards.purpose: The pipeline: validate unique ids, render diagrams up front, dispatch each block to its renderer, then wrap the fragments in an HTML shell with inlined CSS + viewer JS. `html.ts` supplies the escaping and safe-href guards the whole engine shares.

cards.name: renderers
cards.purpose: Nine pure `Block → HTML` functions, one per non-container type. `assemble`'s `switch` calls the right one; groups and tabs recurse back through `assemble`. Markdown is the shared substrate the prose/diff/overview renderers lean on.

cards.name: highlight
cards.purpose: Shiki-backed syntax highlighting behind a lazy singleton. `highlightCode` returns a full `<pre>`; `highlightLines` returns per-line inner HTML for the gutter-aligned diff and annotated-code grids. Both degrade to escaped plaintext on any failure.

cards.name: lint
cards.purpose: Non-blocking authoring guards. Walks the `Block[]` and warns — never throws — when a group lacks a description, a diff description is a wall of text, a diagram applies no color, an editable kind drops its mermaid, or there are too many / oversize diagrams.

cards.name: version
cards.purpose: The provenance stamp: `visual-skills@<git-sha>[-dirty] · <ISO timestamp>`, stamped into the page `<meta generator>` and footer. Falls back to the package.json version, then `"unknown"`, when git is unavailable.

blocks.title: How a document is assembled
blocks.intro: A product hands `assemble()` a `Block[]`. It validates ids, renders every diagram up front via the `diagrams` domain, then dispatches each block to its renderer and wraps the result in the `html.ts` shell. `highlight` and `lint` are helpers along the way; `version` stamps the page.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece — what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the engine and the products that call it.

components.name: block-model
detail: `Block` is a discriminated union keyed on `type` — `diagram`, `schema`, `api`, `file-tree`, `diff`, `prose`, `annotated-code`, `questions`, `group`, `tabs`, `overview`. This is the entire contract between a product and the engine: a generator never touches HTML, it only constructs typed blocks. `assemble`'s exhaustive `switch (b.type)` is checked against this union, so adding a variant is a compile error until a renderer handles it.

Two variants are containers that nest **one level deep**: `group` (a labelled `<details>` of child blocks) and `tabs` (a CSS-only switcher, max 6 tabs). `diff` and `overview` may each carry an optional embedded `diagram`. The only logic here is `isDiagramBlock`, the type guard `assemble` and `lint` use to find every `diagram`/`schema` block — including those nested inside groups, tabs, diffs, and overviews — so they can be rendered through the diagrams domain up front.

files.name: blocks.ts
files.desc: The `Block` union, every variant interface, and the `isDiagramBlock` guard.

exports.name: Block
exports.desc: The discriminated union every product builds and `assemble` walks.

exports.name: DiagramBlock · DiffBlock · ProseBlock · ApiBlock · …
exports.desc: One interface per renderable variant, keyed by `type`.

exports.name: isDiagramBlock()
exports.desc: Type guard — is this block rendered through the d2/mermaid diagram path?

connections.body: atlas-page blocks (`src/atlas-blocks.ts`) compile down to `DiagramBlock` via this same model

components.name: assemble + shell
detail: `assemble(blocks, opts)` is the one entry point every product calls. It first asserts every block id is unique recursively (anchors and in-page `#cross-links` depend on it), runs `lintBlocks` through `opts.onWarn`, then collects every diagram/schema block — including nested ones — and renders them up front through the diagrams domain, keyed by id. It then maps each top-level block to HTML via `renderBlock`, a `switch (b.type)` that calls the matching renderer; `group` and `tabs` recurse. Finally it inlines `assets/template.css` and `assets/viewer.js`, stamps an optional `<meta generator>` and footer, and returns a single self-contained HTML string — no external assets, by design.

`html.ts` is the tiny shared safety layer: `escapeHtml` (used by every renderer for any text that reaches HTML) and `SAFE_HREF`, the regex that allows only `#fragment` and `http(s)://` links so a `javascript:`/`data:` URL can't slip through the overview and review link renderers. `assemble` also injects each block's id as an anchor on its `<section>` so the cross-links resolve.

files.name: assemble.ts
files.desc: Id validation, diagram pre-render, the `renderBlock` dispatch switch, and the HTML shell.

files.name: html.ts
files.desc: `escapeHtml` and the `SAFE_HREF` link-scheme allowlist shared across renderers.

exports.name: assemble()
exports.desc: → `Promise<string>` — `Block[]` + opts to one self-contained HTML document.

exports.name: AssembleOpts · DocStatus
exports.desc: Title, source, optional status banner, outDir, excalidraw, onWarn, generator.

exports.name: escapeHtml() · SAFE_HREF
exports.desc: HTML-text escaping and the link-scheme guard every renderer reuses.

components.name: renderers
detail: Nine pure functions, one per non-container block type — `renderProse`, `renderDiff`, `renderApi`, `renderFileTree`, `renderAnnotatedCode`, `renderOverview`, `renderQuestions`, `renderLegend`, plus the shared `renderMarkdown`. Each takes its block (and an optional `onWarn`) and returns an HTML fragment wrapped in a `vs-block` section; `assemble` adds the anchor and stitches them together. There is no shared base class — the union + the dispatch switch are the only structure.

`markdown.ts` is the substrate: `renderMarkdown` parses GitHub-flavored markdown, highlights fenced code via the highlight module, and runs the result through `sanitize-html` with a strict tag/attribute allowlist; `renderInlineMarkdown` does the one-line variant for headlines and bullets. `prose`, `diff`, and `overview` all build on it. `diff` and `annotated-code` use `highlightLines` to align highlighted code to a gutter; `file-tree` collapses single-child directory chains and links filenames back to their diff blocks; `legend` paints the auto-derived diagram color key.

files.name: markdown.ts
files.desc: `renderMarkdown` / `renderInlineMarkdown` — GFM + Shiki + sanitize-html. The shared substrate.

files.name: prose.ts · overview.ts · questions.ts
files.desc: Narrative blocks: prose body, the TL;DR headline + points, and Q&A cards.

files.name: api.ts · file-tree.ts · legend.ts
files.desc: The tRPC procedure table, the change tree, and the diagram color key.

exports.name: renderMarkdown() · renderInlineMarkdown()
exports.desc: Sanitized GFM → HTML; the substrate the prose/diff/overview renderers reuse.

exports.name: renderProse · renderDiff · renderApi · renderFileTree · renderOverview · renderQuestions · renderAnnotatedCode · renderLegend
exports.desc: One pure `Block → HTML` function per non-container type.

connections.body: [assemble()](#c-assemble) — one per block `type` from the dispatch switch
[highlight](#c-highlight) for code spans; [html.ts](#c-assemble) `escapeHtml` for all text

components.name: highlight
detail: A thin wrapper over Shiki (`github-light` theme, a fixed set of loaded languages) behind a lazy promise singleton, so the highlighter is created at most once per process. `highlightCode` returns a complete Shiki `<pre>` for fenced markdown code; `highlightLines` returns the inner HTML of each source line so `diff` and `annotated-code` can interleave code with gutters and per-line notes. `langFromPath` maps a file extension to a Shiki language id.

Every path is defensive: an unloaded language, a Shiki error, or a line-count mismatch degrades to escaped plaintext (and an `onWarn` note) rather than throwing — a highlighting failure must never break a document.

exports.name: highlightCode()
exports.desc: → a full highlighted `<pre>`, or escaped plain `<pre>` on failure.

exports.name: highlightLines()
exports.desc: → per-line inner HTML (or `null`) for gutter-aligned grids.

exports.name: langFromPath()
exports.desc: File extension → Shiki language id; unknown → `"text"`.

connections.body: [renderers](#c-renderers) — markdown fenced code, diff hunks, annotated code
escaped plaintext via [html.ts](#c-assemble) `escapeHtml` on any failure

components.name: lint
detail: `lintBlocks(blocks)` returns authoring warnings in document order — it never throws and never blocks rendering. `assemble` pipes them to `opts.onWarn`. The guards nudge quality: a `group` with no description, a `diff` description that is a long single paragraph (a wall of text), a `diagram` that applies no semantic color, an editable-eligible diagram kind that dropped its mermaid source (silently losing the Excalidraw upgrade), and too-many / oversize-edge diagrams overall.

It walks the same nested structure `assemble` does — groups, tabs, embedded diff/overview diagrams — using the diagram-color helpers to detect which roles a d2/mermaid source applies. The intent is to catch the mistakes that make a document read worse without making the build fail.

files.name: lint-blocks.ts
files.desc: The group / diff-prose / diagram authoring guards, recursing through nested blocks.

exports.name: lintBlocks()
exports.desc: → `string[]` of non-blocking authoring warnings in document order.

connections.body: diagram color roles via the [diagrams](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html) color helpers

components.name: version
detail: `generatorStamp()` produces the provenance string `visual-skills@<git-short-sha>[-dirty] · <ISO timestamp>`. It shells out to git for the short SHA and a porcelain status (the `-dirty` suffix when the tree has uncommitted changes); if git is unavailable it falls back to the package.json version, then to `"unknown"`. Products pass the result as `opts.generator`, which `assemble` writes into the page `<meta generator>` and footer so any saved document records exactly which build produced it.

files.name: version.ts
files.desc: `generatorStamp` — git SHA / dirty / timestamp with package.json fallback.

exports.name: generatorStamp()
exports.desc: → `Promise<string>` provenance stamp for the page meta + footer.

blocks.title: The block model it owns
blocks.intro: Core owns the `Block` discriminated union — the type model every product builds against and the engine renders. These are the variants it defines.

rows.name: Block
rows.desc: The discriminated union itself — the contract between products and `assemble()`.

rows.name: DiagramBlock · SchemaBlock
rows.desc: A d2 (+ optional mermaid) diagram, and the d2-only ERD schema variant.

rows.name: ApiBlock
rows.desc: A tRPC procedure table — name, kind, auth, input, and change marker per row.

rows.name: DiffBlock
rows.desc: A file diff: path, optional markdown description, optional embedded diagram, and hunks.

rows.name: FileTreeBlock
rows.desc: A change tree of `FileChange` entries, linkable back to their diff blocks.

rows.name: ProseBlock · OverviewBlock
rows.desc: A markdown prose section, and the TL;DR headline + key points (with optional lead diagram).

rows.name: AnnotatedCodeBlock
rows.desc: A code listing with per-line margin notes, gutter-aligned to highlighted source.

rows.name: QuestionsBlock
rows.desc: Open-question cards, each with a recommended default.

rows.name: GroupBlock · TabsBlock
rows.desc: The two one-level-deep containers: a labelled `<details>` group and a CSS-only tab switcher.

blocks.note: All HTML is produced at render time and inlined — core owns no persisted data, only the in-memory block types and the document string `assemble()` returns.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: A small typed surface: products build blocks and call one `assemble()`; core in turn calls the diagrams domain to render every diagram up front.
blocks.note: Called by [recap](../domain-recap/domain-recap.html), [atlas](../domain-atlas/domain-atlas.html), and [authoring](../domain-authoring/domain-authoring.html) — all three build a `Block[]` and hand it to `assemble()`. Core's only dependency is [diagrams](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html): `assemble` renders every diagram/schema block through it before dispatching the rest.
